node.algorithm: Verify P1v4 L2 actuation, B2.1 falsification and B2.2 groundwork; require real Gate-0 acceptance before fast-bond plasticity; expose an ordered dependency receipt without executing or judging experiments.
node.author: codex-hswm-next-gates
node.comment: Fail-closed engineering stage harness for HSWM P1v5/B2.2/F1/P2/P3/P4; no scientific result or prediction.

questions.body: After real Gate-0 pack acceptance, can outcome-bound used-bond eligibility produce a fast query-bond candidate that survives fresh/retention/canary controls, distills into a durable slow Delta-ell, changes later frozen behavior, and loses that gain when the promoted rewrite is removed, while static-update, random-credit, shuffled-eligibility, no-promotion, and equal-budget flat/vector controls fail?

Can at least three typed LLM function roles composed through one HSWM state outperform an equal-call, equal-token single-LLM workflow and vector memory on sealed tasks, with role removal and role shuffle demonstrating that the function topology rather than extra calls carries the effect?
